title: Enjoy Reading vs Want to Read
description: Some verbs take -ING (gerund). Others take TO + base (infinitive). The groups you just have to learn.
summary_pt_br: Verbos seguidos de GERÚNDIO (-ING): enjoy, avoid, finish, keep, mind, suggest, practice. Verbos seguidos de INFINITIVO (TO + base): want, hope, decide, promise, plan, need, learn. Alguns aceitam os dois com sentido quase igual (start, begin, love, like, hate).

scenes.text_en:
'I ENJOY CODING.' (gerund)
'I WANT TO CODE.' (infinitive)
'She SUGGESTED TAKING a break.' (gerund)
'She DECIDED TO TAKE a break.' (infinitive)
The verb BEFORE decides the form AFTER.
scenes.text_pt:
'Eu CURTO PROGRAMAR.' (gerúndio com enjoy)
'Eu QUERO PROGRAMAR.' (infinitivo com want)
'Ela SUGERIU FAZER uma pausa.' (gerúndio)
'Ela DECIDIU FAZER uma pausa.' (infinitivo)
O verbo ANTES decide a forma DEPOIS.

scenes.title: The two lists
scenes.body_en: GERUND (-ING): enjoy, avoid, finish, keep, mind, suggest, practice, consider, miss, admit. 'I enjoy READING.' INFINITIVE (TO + base): want, hope, decide, promise, plan, need, learn, try, offer, agree. 'I want TO READ.' BOTH: start, begin, continue, love, like, hate, prefer. 'I love reading = I love to read.'
scenes.body_pt: GERÚNDIO (-ING): enjoy, avoid, finish, keep, mind, suggest, practice, consider. INFINITIVO (TO + base): want, hope, decide, promise, plan, need, learn. Os dois funcionam: start, begin, continue, love, like, hate.

exercise.explanation: Five verbs, three categories. Memorize as chunks.

exercise.question: Complete: "I enjoy learning new ___."
exercise.explanation: The full phrase is: "I enjoy learning new languages."

exercise.question: Pick the best response. (WANT TO + base.)
options: I want to travel to Europe.
exercise.explanation: The correct response is: "I want to travel to Europe."

scenes.title: Life goals interview
scenes.pt_summary: Gerúndio vs infinitivo — enjoy doing, decide to do, want to.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What do you want to achieve this year?
turns.pt: O que quer alcançar esse ano?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I want to travel to Europe.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Have you decided to start anything new?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, I decided to start yoga.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Do you avoid speaking English in public?
turns.pt: Você evita falar inglês em público?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, I avoid speaking because I am shy.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Good advice. I will keep trying.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: You will grow if you keep going.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Enjoy Reading vs Want to Read". Some verbs take -ING (gerund). Others take TO + base (infinitive). The groups you just have to learn. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Enjoy Reading vs Want to Read". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
Enjoy Reading vs Want to Read
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Enjoy Reading vs Want to Read'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Enjoy Reading vs Want to Read".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: Perfect English Grammar
sources.pt_hint: Regras de gramática + exercícios interativos gratuitos.

sources.label: British Council · A2
